evals.prompt: i have a nuxt 3 app with @clerk/nuxt installed. i need to protect my /dashboard route so only signed-in users can access it. unauthenticated users should be redirected to the sign-in page.
evals.expected_output: Adds definePageMeta middleware auth guard to dashboard page, or creates a route middleware using clerkMiddleware
expectations: Uses definePageMeta({ middleware: 'auth' }) on the dashboard page OR creates a named route middleware
Handles redirect for unauthenticated users
Does NOT use client-side only checks for route protection
Imports are correct for @clerk/nuxt
Does not break the existing app structure

evals.prompt: i need to create a server API route in my nuxt app that returns the current user's data. the route should only work for authenticated users and return 401 for unauthenticated requests.
evals.expected_output: Creates a Nitro server route using event.context.auth or clerkClient to get the current user
expectations: Creates a file in server/api/ directory
Accesses auth state via event.context.auth or imports clerkClient from @clerk/nuxt/server
Returns 401 status for unauthenticated requests
Returns user data for authenticated requests
Uses defineEventHandler from Nitro

evals.prompt: i want to show user profile information on my nuxt page - the user's name, email, and avatar. how do i access this data in my page component?
evals.expected_output: Uses useUser() composable to access user data in the Vue component
expectations: Uses useUser() composable (auto-imported by @clerk/nuxt)
Accesses user.firstName, user.lastName, user.emailAddresses, or user.imageUrl
Handles the loading state before rendering user data
Does NOT make a server fetch for user data when composables suffice
Component uses <script setup> syntax

evals.prompt: my nuxt saas app needs organization support. users should be able to switch between their organizations in the header. show me how to add org switching and display org-scoped content on the dashboard.
evals.expected_output: Adds OrganizationSwitcher component, reads orgId from useAuth() to scope dashboard content
expectations: Adds <OrganizationSwitcher /> component to the header or layout
Uses orgId from useAuth() composable to scope data
Handles case where no org is selected (prompts user to select or create one)
Does NOT remove existing user authentication logic
Dashboard content is conditional on orgId being present

evals.prompt: i want to replace the hosted sign-in page with a custom one inside my nuxt app. show me how to create a custom sign-in page using clerk components.
evals.expected_output: Creates a pages/sign-in.vue page with <SignIn /> component and configures the sign-in URL
expectations: Creates a sign-in page in the pages/ directory
Renders the <SignIn /> component from @clerk/nuxt
Configures NUXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL or signInUrl in nuxt.config.ts
Does NOT implement a custom auth form from scratch
Page is publicly accessible (no auth middleware on it)

evals.prompt: i need to protect a nitro server route that handles payments. the route needs to verify the user is authenticated and get their userId to record the payment in my database.
evals.expected_output: Server route using event.context.auth to get userId, returns 401 if not authenticated
expectations: Uses event.context.auth.userId in a Nitro server route
Returns createError({ statusCode: 401 }) or setResponseStatus(event, 401) for unauthenticated
Extracts userId from auth context to use in database operation
File is placed in server/api/ or server/routes/
Does NOT re-verify the session token manually

evals.prompt: i'm adding clerk to my nuxt 3 app. set up the module and protect the /dashboard route so unauthenticated users are redirected to /sign-in.
evals.expected_output: Adds @clerk/nuxt to modules in nuxt.config.ts, creates Nuxt route middleware that checks auth and redirects
expectations: Adds @clerk/nuxt to the modules array in nuxt.config.ts
Creates a route middleware that checks authentication
Uses useAuth composable inside the middleware
Redirects unauthenticated users to /sign-in via navigateTo
Applies the middleware to the /dashboard route

evals.prompt: my nuxt app has a server API route /api/user-data that should only work for authenticated users. implement the server-side auth check.
evals.expected_output: Uses getAuth from @clerk/nuxt/server in the event handler, throws 401 when userId is falsy
expectations: Imports getAuth from @clerk/nuxt/server
Calls getAuth(event) to get userId
Throws createError({ statusCode: 401 }) when userId is falsy
Uses userId in the data fetching logic
Does NOT use client-side composables in the server route

evals.prompt: i need to add a global nuxt server middleware that checks auth on all protected routes and redirects to /sign-in. use clerkMiddleware from @clerk/nuxt.
evals.expected_output: Creates server/middleware/clerk.ts with clerkMiddleware that checks H3Event context for auth
expectations: Creates server/middleware/clerk.ts
Uses clerkMiddleware from @clerk/nuxt/server
Exports the result as default
Can access auth state via event.context.auth
Does NOT create the middleware in middleware/ (that is for route middleware, not server middleware)
